item.title: This just feels unreal now
item.description: Pete Souza published this photo titled "Rain and the Rose Garden”, with the caption, "When the skies opened up one fall afternoon, I saw the President reach out to feel the raindrops". The image of President Barack Obama standing on the Colonnade of the White House, looking out at the Rose Garden was captured on October 15, 2014, when a sudden rainstorm began.
item.dc:creator: CaptainBackPain

item.title: Super Ultra Mega Cosplay Roulette
item.description: Let the wheel spin! Where it stops, that’s your GF :P I’ve done over 300 cosplays in my career... That is so many that only half of them are represented in this video… Hard to grasp. Also, I've never done this here in my 12 years on Imgur, but if you're interested in supporting me, consider my Patreon. I have a couple reasonably priced tiers plus you get immediate access to all 300 of those extensive cosplay sets plus… so much more I cannot speak of here… https://www.patreon.com/AlsoAshley Anyway, thank you all for allowing me to make this my career :D Let me know who you get!
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: Unfit For Public Service
item.description: This is Los Angeles City Attorney Hydee Feldstein Soto. Three days before the 2nd No Kings Protest, she filed a motion to lift an injunction which disallows city police from using force on members of the press. She did this on the behalf of LAPD and without consulting Los Angeles City Council, then she ignored city council when they voted unanimously for the LA City Attorney's Office to withdraw the motion. Her request for reporters to be pepper sprayed, beaten, and zip-tied for doing their jobs was ultimately blocked by a judge a day before the protest was scheduled. Angelenos, when this unAmerican boot-licking sack of pig shit runs for city attorney again on June 2, 2026, vote her fascist ass out. https://laist.com/news/politics/judge-blocks-lapd-request-use-of-force-press-no-kings-protests?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_content=&utm_campaign=LAist&fbclid=IwVERDUANne0JleHRuA2FlbQIxMQABHtkMaMWBgBfvCLVR8BqAAm1O9x_apePMpd7XZn0FBqpRYhy1vbhJOL4IZNsN_aem_CjbD0RBBT4eYWvcT9zVLNQ
item.dc:creator: Tomcat1982

item.title: I quit my job to do my recipe websites full time!!! Nervous happy and excited!!
item.description:
And I had a photoshoot to celebrate!! Thank you Imgur for all your support and engagement with my recipes. I’ve been creating recipes for triedandtruerecipe.com for almost 10 years while working full-time. If you didn’t know, I’m a design director by day and the job has just gotten so stressful and exhausting that I felt like I couldn’t devote the kind of attention to my recipes like I wanted to. I’ve been working nonstop — late nights, weekends, and I just got severely burnt out. I finally made the decision to submit my notice and I’m so excited to have more time to create new recipes and share them with you!!!!
And we had a shoot for my best friend’s cake idea that she gave me — emmy’s espresso martini upside down cake! Recipe below ? https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/emmys-espresso-martini-upside-down-cake/ Coffee-caramel layer: 4 tablespoons butter ½ cup light brown sugar 2 teaspoons instant espresso powder 3 tablespoons heavy cream Batter: 1 cup cake flour 1 teaspoon baking powder ¼ teaspoon kosher salt 4 tablespoons butter (room temperature) ½ cup light brown sugar 1 egg 1½ ounces freshly brewed espresso (see Note 1) ½ cup whole milk 1 teaspoon vanilla extract Whipped Kahlúa Mascarpone ½ cup heavy cream ½ cup powdered sugar ¼ cup Kahlúa (or your favorite coffee liqueur) 1 teaspoon vanilla extract 4 ounces mascarpone (set out at room temperature for 15 minutes) For serving: Chocolate-covered espresso beans Instant espresso powder Make the caramel: Preheat the oven to 350ºF. Lightly grease a 9-inch cake pan. Set aside. Melt 4 tablespoons of butter in a small pot over medium heat. Once melted, add ½ cup light brown sugar and 2 teaspoons instant espresso powder. Stir to combine. Cook over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture darkens slightly in color. Remove from the heat. Add the heavy cream and whisk until smooth. Pour the mixture into the prepared cake pan and smooth it into an even layer. Transfer to the refrigerator for 15 minutes. Make the batter: Sift 1 cup cake flour, 1 teaspoon baking powder, and ¼ teaspoon kosher salt into a small bowl. Set aside. Add 4 tablespoons of butter and ½ cup light brown sugar to a large bowl. Use an electric hand mixer to cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Add 1 egg and mix until smooth. Add half the sifted dry ingredients and beat with the mixer until just combined. Add the brewed espresso and half the milk and beat until incorporated. Add the remaining dry ingredients and the remaining milk, along with 1 teaspoon vanilla extract. Beat until just combined. Don’t over-mix! Assemble the cake: Remove the cake pan from the refrigerator. Pour the batter over the caramel and carefully smooth it into an even layer. Bake the upside-down cake: Transfer the cake to the oven for 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean. Let the cake stand for about 15 to 20 minutes at room temperature. See Note 2. Prepare the Kahlúa whipped mascarpone: While the cake cools, prepare the frosting. Place the ½ cup heavy cream in a large bowl. Add the ½ cup powdered sugar, ¼ cup Kahlúa, and 1 teaspoon vanilla extract. Beat with an electric mixer until soft peaks form. Add the mascarpone cheese and continue beating until stiff peaks form. Refrigerate until needed. Invert the cake: Place a large plate over the cake pan and carefully flip the cake onto the plate. Use a small butter knife to tap over the pan to encourage the release of the cake. Allow the cake to cool completely. Frost the cake: Transfer the whipped mascarpone to a piping bag with a star tip. Pipe the frosting over the cake. Arrange chocolate-covered espresso beans on top of the frosting and dust with more instant espresso powder, if desired. Slice the cake into pieces and enjoy! Notes Note 1: If you don’t have an espresso machine, dissolve 1½ teaspoons in 1½ ounces of warm water. Note 2: This caramel is quite thick, so if you allow it to cool completely, it won’t release easily from the pan. You should be able to handle the pan with your hands, but it should be warm to the touch. If the cake cools completely, you can reheat the bottom briefly over a burner on the stove or even with a torch if you have one. I’ve even used a candle lighter before.
item.dc:creator: triedandtruerecipes

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1503 (toxin identification)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
I'm curious what yall feel which toxin I am from the following list.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "okay. Oh Pippin! Oh Pippin. Good boy." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin

item.title: The Baby Raccoon
item.description:
1987-03-09
1987-03-10
1987-03-11
1987-03-12
1987-03-13
1987-03-14
1987-03-16
1987-03-17
1987-03-18 My wife found a dead kitten outside one morning and this story almost wrote itself. Most of my earlier strips had simple gags, so this was a big departure in tone. Death, of course, is not a common subject for a "kid" strip. The story not only revealed new facets of Calvin's personality but it also suggested to me that the strip was broad enough to handle a wide range of subjects, ideas, and emotions. The strip's world suddenly opened up. Bill Watterson
item.dc:creator: LinneaOnTheBeach
